title: Introduce telemetry-driven synthetic load generator and hyperops for batch profile autotuning
preview: Problem: Production telemetry indicates wide variance in per-customer request shape (seq lengths, prompt/templates, temperature) which makes static batching defaults suboptimal. We need a way to synthesize representative load from existing traces, run lightweight microbenchmark passes, and generate conservative batc...
text: Problem: Production telemetry indicates wide variance in per-customer request shape (seq lengths, prompt/templates, temperature) which makes static batching defaults suboptimal. We need a way to synthesize representative load from existing traces, run lightweight microbenchmark passes, and generate conservative batch/cache/quantization profiles that are safe for rollout. This PR implements a telemetry-driven 'synthetic-econ-proxy' tool plus runtime hyperops that drive an autotuner. Goals: 1) generate reproducible synthetic streams from sampled traces, 2) run a fast hyperparameter sweep for batch sizes, microbatch windows, and cache TTLs in a sandbox mode, 3) attach a compact 'handoff' metadata blob to candidate profiles for safe staged rollout and automatic rollback. Key features: - synthetic-econ-proxy CLI: consumes sampled telemetry (p50/p95 seq distribution, client headers, model f...

title: Self-healing traffic gate with prognostic rollback scheduler
preview: Motivation: We observed incident patterns where a small regression in an A/B arm degraded user experience slowly and only surfaced after sustained traffic. This PR adds a prognostic rollback scheduler and a self-healing traffic gate that combines short-horizon telemetry forecasting, a conservative anomaly scorer, an...
text: Motivation: We observed incident patterns where a small regression in an A/B arm degraded user experience slowly and only surfaced after sustained traffic. This PR adds a prognostic rollback scheduler and a self-healing traffic gate that combines short-horizon telemetry forecasting, a conservative anomaly scorer, and lightweight replay/bisect workflows so the system can proactively hold or revert risky promotes before widespread impact. Summary of changes: implemented a prognostic score pipeline that consumes recent SLO/time-series windows and outputs a short-term risk forecast; added TrafficGate controller that enforces holdpoints when forecasted risk crosses policy thresholds; built a rescue-oracle that can run fast replay of recent 1% samples in a sandbox to validate rollback decisions; added operator-visible "rescue" and "replay" actions to Redwood Console so on-call can review di...

title: per-request graceful-degradation: kernel compatibility shim + precision-hint API
preview: Motivation: We continue to see tail latency regressions in mixed-precision kernels when models with large kv-cache footprints are routed through the 'fast' execution path. This PR introduces a lightweight compatibility shim and a per-request 'precision hint' header that lets the router/scheduler choose a conservativ...
text:
Motivation: We continue to see tail latency regressions in mixed-precision kernels when models with large kv-cache footprints are routed through the 'fast' execution path. This PR introduces a lightweight compatibility shim and a per-request 'precision hint' header that lets the router/scheduler choose a conservative execution mode for requests that prioritize correctness over absolute token throughput.

What this PR does (single-string description): Adds a kernel-compat shim layer in the runtime that detects binary incompatibility or quantization sensitivity for a model+request combination and exposes a low-overhead boolean that the scheduler uses to avoid aggressive fusion/quantization fast-paths; implements an opt-in HTTP header (X-Redwood-Precision-Hint: prefer-correct | prefer-fast | neutral) and SDK flags that map to the same hint; extends continuous-batching logic to respect pe...

title: Probabilistic length cohorter with pad-aware scheduling and KV-prefill heuristics
preview: Motivation: long-tail sequence length variance continues to drive excessive padding and suboptimal kernel utilization in our continuous batching pipeline. Simple bucketing reduces padding but creates brittle boundaries and poor utilization when per-slot latency SLOs are tight. This PR introduces a probabilistic leng...
text:
Motivation: long-tail sequence length variance continues to drive excessive padding and suboptimal kernel utilization in our continuous batching pipeline. Simple bucketing reduces padding but creates brittle boundaries and poor utilization when per-slot latency SLOs are tight. This PR introduces a probabilistic length cohorter that (1) forms soft cohorts by sampling length-affinity probabilities instead of hard buckets, (2) attaches a pad-cost estimate to each pending request using a small learned linear cost model, and (3) integrates KV-prefill priority to bias cohorts toward requests that benefit from warm KV caches. The goal is to reduce aggregate padding waste while keeping P95 latency within SLO and increasing kernel lane alignment for improved throughput.

Approach: the cohorter computes a length-affinity vector for each request based on requested max_tokens, recent sequence len...

title: silence-probe-reconnect-backfill-and-cancel-deadline-enforcement
preview: Motivation: Customers observed client disconnects that left server-side streaming state in a limbo where tool-calls were retried and KV-cache backfills were skipped, causing higher token costs and functional duplication. This PR introduces a 'silence probe' reconciliation path, a reconnect probe that attempts lightw...
text: Motivation: Customers observed client disconnects that left server-side streaming state in a limbo where tool-calls were retried and KV-cache backfills were skipped, causing higher token costs and functional duplication. This PR introduces a 'silence probe' reconciliation path, a reconnect probe that attempts lightweight session health checks on client reconnects, an explicit cancel-deadline enforcement for in-flight tool/function calls, and safer backfill semantics when reconnect resumes. Summary of changes: 1) server: add silence-probe detection that marks streams as 'candidate-orphan' after configurable idle windows and enqueues a lightweight snapshot probe before evicting; 2) reconnect: on new client handshake include reconnect-probe phase which validates KV-cache continuity and replays missing prefix tokens if safe; 3) cancel-deadline: tool invocation subsystem now tracks cancel ...

title: ack-window-coalescing-and-reconnect-probe-for-resumable-streams
preview: Context: We have had intermittent production incidents where client disconnect followed by rapid reconnects created duplicate tool/function calls or left server-side inflight work stranded. This PR introduces a lightweight client ACK window and a reconnect probe handshake to make streaming sessions more resilient to...
text:
Context: We have had intermittent production incidents where client disconnect followed by rapid reconnects created duplicate tool/function calls or left server-side inflight work stranded. This PR introduces a lightweight client ACK window and a reconnect probe handshake to make streaming sessions more resilient to flapping connections while preserving OpenAI compatibility for client-driven cancels.

Goals:
- Allow the client to acknowledge receipt of terminal tokens (finalized structured output / function call invocation) so the server can safely drop inflight state sooner and avoid double-execution on reconnects.
- Coalesce rapid client cancel/soft-abort signals within a short ACK window to reduce noisy cancellation churn during flaky networks.
- Add a reconnect probe (small idempotency-aware handshake) that validates resume tokens and server lease state before resuming expensive w...

title: model-catalog: add filters, compatibility flags, and pricing/limits fields to /models API
preview: Summary: Adds richer filtering and capability metadata to the public model catalog endpoints. This PR introduces query filters for capability-based discovery and model attributes, plus explicit compatibility flags and first-class pricing/limits fields on model descriptors. Motivation: customers have asked for a mach...
text: Summary: Adds richer filtering and capability metadata to the public model catalog endpoints. This PR introduces query filters for capability-based discovery and model attributes, plus explicit compatibility flags and first-class pricing/limits fields on model descriptors. Motivation: customers have asked for a machine-readable way to pick models by capability (e.g., tool-calling, structured output, streaming-stable) and to programmatically surface cost/limit information for routing and billing. The current /models response is sparse and requires ad-hoc parsing of descriptions in downstream services. What this changes: 1) API surface: GET /v1/models now accepts new query params: 'capability' (comma-separated values: chat, completion, embedding, tool_calling, structured_output, streaming), 'openai_compat' (true|false), 'pricing_tier' (free|standard|premium), and 'min_max_tokens' (min,m...

title: Introduce kernel-level heatmap and cost-flow annotator for invocation traces
preview: Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to surface GPU/kernel-level queuing and contention hotspots correlated to token-level cost and per-stage latency. This PR adds a sampled, lightweight kernel-heatmap export and a cost-flow annotator that stitches stage timing with kernel occupancy to help SREs and runtime engine...
text: Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to surface GPU/kernel-level queuing and contention hotspots correlated to token-level cost and per-stage latency. This PR adds a sampled, lightweight kernel-heatmap export and a cost-flow annotator that stitches stage timing with kernel occupancy to help SREs and runtime engineers diagnose expensive invocation patterns. Design summary: 1) A new kernel heatmap sampler emits compact histograms of kernel queue wait and execution time per-device and per-invocation-phase; sampling is prioritized for requests that exceed a configurable latency or cost threshold. 2) A cost-flow annotator postprocesses traces to attach a per-invocation-stage cost attribution vector (model load, key-value cache miss penalties, kernel stalls, token-generation time) and synthesizes rollup markers used by dashboards. 3) Minimal on-path overhead: sampler uses atomic ring-buff...

title: per-tenant throughput SLA enforcer + shared burst token refactor
preview: Motivation: customers on Dedicated ask for predictable per-tenant throughput guarantees while still allowing short-term bursts across a shared fleet. This PR implements an in-runtime per-tenant throughput SLA enforcer plus a refactor of the fleet-wide shared burst token pool to support weighted allocation and backpr...
text:
Motivation: customers on Dedicated ask for predictable per-tenant throughput guarantees while still allowing short-term bursts across a shared fleet. This PR implements an in-runtime per-tenant throughput SLA enforcer plus a refactor of the fleet-wide shared burst token pool to support weighted allocation and backpressure coordination with the autoscaler.

Summary of changes: added a light-weight controller (runtime/fleet/sla_enforcer.go) that tracks per-tenant token budgets (tokens/sec) and allocates from the local host bucket first, then from the shared burst pool when available. The shared burst pool has been moved out of the legacy global dispatcher and into a refactored service (runtime/fleet/burst_pool.go) that exposes an async lease API and utilization metrics. The autoscaler now consumes burst-pool pressure signals to pre-warm capacity when pool exhaustion is sustained. Consol...

title: add-invoke-slice-sampler-and-token-cascade-explainer
preview: Motivation: long-tail token cost and latency noise make it hard to correlate bursts to kernel warmup, prefix vs completion cost, and scheduling contention. This PR introduces two complementary observability primitives: (1) an Invoke Slice Sampler that slices invocations into time-aligned 'slices' to preserve bursty ...
text: Motivation: long-tail token cost and latency noise make it hard to correlate bursts to kernel warmup, prefix vs completion cost, and scheduling contention. This PR introduces two complementary observability primitives: (1) an Invoke Slice Sampler that slices invocations into time-aligned 'slices' to preserve bursty token-attribution while dramatically reducing cardinality and storage cost; and (2) a Token Cascade Explainer that attributes per-token cost/latency to a small set of causal contributors (prefix KV hits/misses, kernel warmup penalty, batching wait, and I/O stalls) and exposes an explainer panel in the console. Summary of changes: - New sampler: telemetry/ingest/slice_sampler.go adds a deterministic slice keyed by (tenant, route, invocation_bucket) and emits sampled span anchors plus compact slice digests. Sampler is opt-in via telemetry.config.slice_sampling.enabled=false|t...

title: Orchestra pausepoint harness with transparent rewind and cohort quarantine
preview:
Motivation:
We need a deterministic way to hold, inspect, and safely rewind model rollout traffic for live canaries and A/B experiments without losing session-affinity or producing silent regressions. Current pause mechanics are ad-hoc (manual scaling, pod cordon) and opaque in telemetry which makes post-hoc root ca...
text:
Motivation:
We need a deterministic way to hold, inspect, and safely rewind model rollout traffic for live canaries and A/B experiments without losing session-affinity or producing silent regressions. Current pause mechanics are ad-hoc (manual scaling, pod cordon) and opaque in telemetry which makes post-hoc root cause and safe rewind labor-intensive.

What this PR does (summary):
- Introduces a Pausepoint state machine inside the orchestrator that exposes explicit holdpoints for staged rollouts. Holdpoints can be triggered by policy (telemetry gates) or manually from the Console.
- Implements a Cohort Quarantine controller that isolates affected user slices when a holdpoint is entered; quarantined cohorts are routed to a shadow or golden variant, and traffic is stamped for replay.
- Adds Transparent Rewind: deterministic session-preserving replay of quarantined traffic against previo...

title: shortburst-attention-fusion-and-slab-packing-for-short-sequence-latency
preview: Motivation: Many customer workloads at Redwood are dominated by short sequences (<64 tokens) where per-request overhead (kernel launch, KV cache management, micro-batch alignment) dominates token cost. This PR introduces a runtime path optimized for short-burst workloads: 1) an attention-fusion kernel variant that f...
text: Motivation: Many customer workloads at Redwood are dominated by short sequences (<64 tokens) where per-request overhead (kernel launch, KV cache management, micro-batch alignment) dominates token cost. This PR introduces a runtime path optimized for short-burst workloads: 1) an attention-fusion kernel variant that fuses small-sequence attention + rotary/ALiBi precompute to reduce kernel launches for sequences <= 64, 2) a token-slab packing allocator that packs multiple short requests into a single device slab with per-request offsets to avoid per-token KV allocations, and 3) a micro-continuous batching tweak that lowers the batching tick for short sequences while preserving overall throughput for long sequences. The goal is to reduce median latency for short requests without regressions for long-tail workloads. Changes: implemented fused_attention_short.cu and refactored attention dis...

title: Add hardware-timestamp tracepoints and synthetic-replay microbench for kernel latency and tile occupancy
preview: Motivation: pinpoint sub-kernel latency variance and per-tile occupancy skew seen during continuous batching on long sequences. This PR adds lightweight hardware-timestamp tracepoints around kernel launches, a small lock-free per-tile occupancy counter, and a deterministic synthetic-replay microbenchmark that replay...
text: Motivation: pinpoint sub-kernel latency variance and per-tile occupancy skew seen during continuous batching on long sequences. This PR adds lightweight hardware-timestamp tracepoints around kernel launches, a small lock-free per-tile occupancy counter, and a deterministic synthetic-replay microbenchmark that replays recorded request traces at controlled concurrency for stable profiling. Changes are gated behind runtime flags so there is zero overhead when disabled. High-level summary: 1) runtime/tracing/hw_timestamp_trace.h/.cc implements RAII-style trace scopes that capture wallclock and GPU hw timestamps when available and annotate the internal timeline with span ids, 2) runtime/scheduler/tile_counter.h/.cc introduces a cache-friendly per-tile occupancy counter sampled by the scheduler to record occupancy distributions, 3) tools/replay_bench parses a compressed request trace and re...

title: Queue-horizon throttling and KV probe backoff for continuous batcher
preview: Motivation: recent production incidents showed pathological tail latency when short-lived high-priority traffic interleaved with KV-heavy long-context requests. The continuous batcher could become congested by probe/burst requests that pin KV slots and prevent fairness tokens from progressing, causing latency spikes...
text: Motivation: recent production incidents showed pathological tail latency when short-lived high-priority traffic interleaved with KV-heavy long-context requests. The continuous batcher could become congested by probe/burst requests that pin KV slots and prevent fairness tokens from progressing, causing latency spikes. This PR introduces a queue-horizon throttling policy and an exponential backoff for KV probe requests to reduce transient pin contention while preserving throughput.

title: Proportional backfill allocator and soft-reservation smoothing for Dedicated fleets
preview: Motivation: Dedicated fleets often have fragmented reserved capacity where tenants underutilize their reservations for short windows. Current behavior returns unused tokens aggressively which leads to oscillation when reservations are re-acquired and contributes to noisy autoscaler decisions. This PR implements a pr...
text:
Motivation: Dedicated fleets often have fragmented reserved capacity where tenants underutilize their reservations for short windows. Current behavior returns unused tokens aggressively which leads to oscillation when reservations are re-acquired and contributes to noisy autoscaler decisions. This PR implements a proportional backfill allocator plus soft-reservation smoothing to allow unused reserved capacity to be temporarily shared across tenants in a controlled, decaying manner.

What this changes (single string): 1) Proportional backfill allocator: when there is spare reserved headroom, we compute per-tenant proportional backfill based on their active demand and reservation weight. Backfill amounts are distributed as fractional credits with deterministic rounding to avoid starvation. Backfill is tagged with ttl and a reclaimable flag so original owners can reclaim capacity within ...

title: introduce-cost-rules-engine-and-autotune-batching-defaults
preview: Summary: Add a Cost Rules Engine that automatically recommends and applies batching and KV-cache defaults per model and workload profile, exposes quantization profile hints for safe auto-application, and introduces a unit-economics estimator in the console and CLI. Motivation: customers routinely ask for hands-off c...
text: Summary: Add a Cost Rules Engine that automatically recommends and applies batching and KV-cache defaults per model and workload profile, exposes quantization profile hints for safe auto-application, and introduces a unit-economics estimator in the console and CLI. Motivation: customers routinely ask for hands-off cost tuning; operational teams want reproducible, audited defaults that reduce per-token costs without surprising latency regressions. This PR bundles runtime heuristics, a small rules DSL, console UI plumbing, and an opt-in rollout for auto-quantization. What changed (high level): - new runtime module redwood/serving/cost_rules.py with rules evaluation, telemetry hooks, and safe-apply gates - batcher tuning extension that selects default batch sizes based on latency SLOs and sequence-length histograms - KV cache policy defaults and prefix cache eligibility heuristics - quan...

title: Add Security FAQ / RFP snippets to Internal Admin (approved-language library + API)
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-class “Security Snippets” support to Internal Admin so we can centrally manage approved FAQ/RFP text blocks (and their versions) that Sales/SE tooling consumes. This is part of the Enterprise Security Enablement Kit refresh: we’ve had repeated drift between Drive/Confluence snippets, HubS...
text: Summary: This PR adds first-class “Security Snippets” support to Internal Admin so we can centrally manage approved FAQ/RFP text blocks (and their versions) that Sales/SE tooling consumes. This is part of the Enterprise Security Enablement Kit refresh: we’ve had repeated drift between Drive/Confluence snippets, HubSpot templates, and ad-hoc copy/paste. The goal is a single source of truth with clear ownership, review metadata, and safe distribution semantics (no restricted evidence in snippets). Motivation / context: Enterprise security reviews are gating Dedicated/Private deals; field teams need fast, consistent answers without over-claiming. Today, approved language lives across Confluence + Drive and gets copied into various tools. This introduces: (1) a canonical snippet store; (2) an API for downstream consumption; (3) RBAC + audit log events for changes; (4) explicit versioning ...

title: SDKS: auth mosaic + streaming consolidation and ergonomic API polish
preview: Context: We observed subtle divergence in how Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs handled token refresh during long-lived streams and how retry presets influence stream resume characteristics. This caused two classes of production issues: (1) opaque reauth races that produced 401s during resume, and (2) inconsistent backpress...
text: Context: We observed subtle divergence in how Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs handled token refresh during long-lived streams and how retry presets influence stream resume characteristics. This caused two classes of production issues: (1) opaque reauth races that produced 401s during resume, and (2) inconsistent backpressure hints leading to latency spikes when batching heuristics changed across SDKs. This PR introduces a small cross-SDK surface (SessionSurface) and a compact "mosaic" of auth utilities to align behavior. Key goals: harmonize reauth handshake, provide ergonomic retry presets (latency-first and throughput-first), and expose minimal stream backpressure hints to SDK consumers. Changes: - Introduce SessionSurface (py) and SessionFactory (go) as a lightweight facade for long-lived sessions; exported helpers in ts for parity. - Add two retry preset bundles: latency_aware and throu...

title: Console: Shadow experiment results page (quality deltas + example drilldowns) behind feature flag
preview:
## Summary
Adds the Console UI for viewing Shadow Experiment results: quality deltas vs control, confidence intervals, segment breakdowns, and a drilldown drawer for scored examples.

This is explicitly *read-only* UI for the beta. Creation flow remains in the existing experiment wizard (DES-611 work), and the resul...
text:
## Summary
Adds the Console UI for viewing Shadow Experiment results: quality deltas vs control, confidence intervals, segment breakdowns, and a drilldown drawer for scored examples.

This is explicitly *read-only* UI for the beta. Creation flow remains in the existing experiment wizard (DES-611 work), and the results page is linked from Rollouts timeline + the Shadow Experiments list.

Everything in this PR is gated behind `shadow_experiments_results_ui` (per tenant). When disabled, routes fall back to the existing placeholder/"Coming soon" page.

## Why
We have shadow traffic shipping in dogfood and early Dedicated beta, but teams can’t validate quality changes without an at-a-glance view of deltas and a way to inspect examples. This PR wires up the results UX to the new aggregates + examples endpoints from the Console backend (PR #24933) and renders:
- Quality delta charts (candida...

title: Audit logs for shadow experiment + shadow policy changes
preview: Summary: Adds audit log entries for shadow experiment lifecycle actions (create/update/archive) and for route-attached shadow policy updates. This is part of Shadow Traffic Quality Metrics beta readiness: we need a clear who/what/when trail for experiment changes and policy toggles, especially for Dedicated/Private ...
text:
Summary: Adds audit log entries for shadow experiment lifecycle actions (create/update/archive) and for route-attached shadow policy updates. This is part of Shadow Traffic Quality Metrics beta readiness: we need a clear who/what/when trail for experiment changes and policy toggles, especially for Dedicated/Private tenants with compliance requirements.

Context / Motivation: Shadow traffic is explicitly “fail-open” and non-user-impacting, but misconfiguration can still create meaningful cost/retention risk (sampling rate, targets, redaction flags). During dogfood and early customer discussions, we’ve been asked to provide auditable records for: (1) who created/changed an experiment, (2) who attached/detached shadow policies to routes/rollouts, and (3) what fields changed. This PR extends the existing AuditLog service to capture the relevant actions and adds minimal schema support for ...

title: Shadow traffic: telemetry events + ingestion hooks (v1)
preview: Context: Shadow traffic routing (ENG-5402) mirrors a sampled subset of prod requests to one or more shadow targets. To compute quality deltas (and to debug routing/scoring lag) we need a low-cardinality, privacy-aware set of telemetry events that (a) links the control and shadow inference outcomes, (b) can be ingest...
text:
Context: Shadow traffic routing (ENG-5402) mirrors a sampled subset of prod requests to one or more shadow targets. To compute quality deltas (and to debug routing/scoring lag) we need a low-cardinality, privacy-aware set of telemetry events that (a) links the control and shadow inference outcomes, (b) can be ingested reliably in Hosted/Dedicated/Private, and (c) provides enough dimensions to slice by route/model/region without exploding metrics cardinality.

This PR introduces shadow traffic telemetry events and wires them into the ingestion pipeline (Kafka -> ingest -> warehouse tables) with initial dashboard stubs for internal dogfood.

What’s included: (1) New event schemas: shadow_pair_created (emitted when the mirror is scheduled), shadow_inference_completed (shadow target returned or timed out), and shadow_scoring_status (async scoring queued/completed/failed). Each event inclu...

title: Add tier/deployment_mode tags to SLI metrics at ingress (and propagate through request context)
preview: Context: As part of the Tiered SLO + Error Budget program, we need to compute SLIs (success rate, latency, streaming reliability) separately for Hosted (shared) vs Dedicated (reserved capacity). Today most gateway metrics are emitted without a reliable tier signal, and routing/fallback can further obscure the origin...
text: Context: As part of the Tiered SLO + Error Budget program, we need to compute SLIs (success rate, latency, streaming reliability) separately for Hosted (shared) vs Dedicated (reserved capacity). Today most gateway metrics are emitted without a reliable tier signal, and routing/fallback can further obscure the original customer tier in downstream services. This PR adds tier/deployment mode tags to ingress SLI metrics and ensures the tags are attached to the request context so they can be propagated into routing, traces, and downstream metrics. Summary of changes: (1) Introduce a canonical tier classifier at ingress that maps requests into tier={hosted|dedicated} and deployment_mode={hosted_api|dedicated_pool} (naming aligned with ADR-0142 draft). (2) Add these tags to the gateway SLI metric set for request count, request duration histograms, and streaming termination counters. (3) Prop...

title: Packed INT8 microkernel rewrite and token coalescer to improve throughput for small-batch inference
preview: Context and motivation: small-batch interactive workloads (chat, per-request short-context generation) show poor INT8 utilization because microkernel packing and per-token scheduling produce many narrow GEMM shapes and high per-call overhead. Prior batching heuristics mitigated this at the request layer but left ine...
text:
Context and motivation: small-batch interactive workloads (chat, per-request short-context generation) show poor INT8 utilization because microkernel packing and per-token scheduling produce many narrow GEMM shapes and high per-call overhead. Prior batching heuristics mitigated this at the request layer but left inefficiencies inside the kernel and scheduling layer. This PR introduces two coordinated changes to address that: (1) a packed INT8 microkernel optimized for narrow micro-tiles and reduced memory traffic, and (2) a runtime token coalescer that merges short token sequences across concurrent requests into aligned micro-batches at the kernel boundary.

Design summary:
- Packed INT8 microkernel: new micro-tiling layout (packed 4x8 major order) that reduces memory stride and enables vector-friendly inner loops; fused requantization step merged into the GEMM write-back to avoid a f...

title: Drift‑resilient collective scheduler and KV slab consolidation to reduce NCCL contention
preview: Context and motivation: In production we continue to see sporadic tail latency spikes on multi‑GPU TP workloads correlated with short windows of NCCL congestion and many small KV cache allocations from continuous batching. This PR introduces two coordinated runtime changes: (1) a drift‑resilient collective scheduler...
text:
Context and motivation: In production we continue to see sporadic tail latency spikes on multi‑GPU TP workloads correlated with short windows of NCCL congestion and many small KV cache allocations from continuous batching. This PR introduces two coordinated runtime changes: (1) a drift‑resilient collective scheduler that perturbs and staggers NCCL launches to avoid pathological synchronization across tensor slices, and (2) a KV slab consolidation pass that reduces small allocation churn and improves L2/L3 locality for the KV cache allocator. The combined change targets multi‑GPU tensor‑parallel inference paths (TP runtime) and the continuous‑batching code path used by the API router.

What this PR does (high level): - Adds Scheduler::PerturbationWindow, a lightweight jittering layer that applies per‑peer launch offsets within a configurable window to break lockstep NCCL launches while...

title: Deprecate legacy stream shims; add credential lookup facade and unify SDK iterables
preview: Context: Multiple SDK consumers reported subtle differences in auth lookup timing and streaming iterator shapes across languages. The legacy 'stream shims' layer introduced in earlier rollouts has become a maintenance burden and makes cross-language parity work harder. Motivation: Reduce duplicated auth lookup code,...
text: Context: Multiple SDK consumers reported subtle differences in auth lookup timing and streaming iterator shapes across languages. The legacy 'stream shims' layer introduced in earlier rollouts has become a maintenance burden and makes cross-language parity work harder. Motivation: Reduce duplicated auth lookup code, surface a single lightweight facade for credential resolution that all SDKs call into, and simplify streaming APIs to a single async iterator/pull-generator mapping with clearer backpressure hints. What this PR does: 1) Adds a Credential Lookup Facade (sdk/common/cred_lookup) that centralizes token resolution, TTL jitter logic, and prefetch hints used by the Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs. 2) Reworks streaming surface so each SDK exports a single ergonomic async/pull iterator that maps to the runtime streaming protocol; legacy shims (StreamShimV1) are deprecated and left ...

title: Coordinated credential handoff, compact retry presets, and generator stream ergonomics across SDKs
preview: Motivation: We keep getting support tickets and cross-SDK bugs around: (1) credential handoff semantics when a streaming connection needs to rotate/refresh tokens mid-session, (2) divergent ergonomic surfaces for generator/async-iterator based streaming in each SDK, and (3) inconsistent retry presets that lead to su...
text:
Motivation: We keep getting support tickets and cross-SDK bugs around: (1) credential handoff semantics when a streaming connection needs to rotate/refresh tokens mid-session, (2) divergent ergonomic surfaces for generator/async-iterator based streaming in each SDK, and (3) inconsistent retry presets that lead to surprising latency/cost tradeoffs in low-latency apps. This PR centralizes the contract in the monorepo and ships coordinated changes for the TypeScript/Python/Go SDKs and runtime float so customers can opt into a single predictable behaviour.

What this PR does (high-level): Add a CredentialHandoff contract (lightweight interface + reference implementation), introduce a new compact retry preset set (compact-1/2/3) tuned for short-sequence, latency-sensitive workloads, and unify the public generator/async-iterator surface across SDKs so streaming ergonomics are consistent and...

title: Holdpoint-carousel phased promote with rehearsal-shadow replay and rollback playbook
preview: Summary: Introduces a holdpoint-driven 'carousel' promote strategy aimed at safe staged promotes across session-sensitive workloads. The feature combines (1) holdpoint anchors — deterministic traffic cut points keyed by session cohorts, (2) a rehearsal shadow runner that replays representative traffic for each holdp...
text:
Summary: Introduces a holdpoint-driven 'carousel' promote strategy aimed at safe staged promotes across session-sensitive workloads. The feature combines (1) holdpoint anchors — deterministic traffic cut points keyed by session cohorts, (2) a rehearsal shadow runner that replays representative traffic for each holdpoint prior to live promotion, and (3) a rollback playbook that encodes automatic and manual abort semantics with quarantine lanes.

Motivation: Multiple incidents where incremental canaries passed aggregate metrics but failed for sticky-session cohorts motivated a promotion strategy that ensures session-coherent rehearsals before traffic cutover. This PR implements the orchestration primitives and a default rollback playbook that operators can tune.

Design:
- Holdpoint controller: computes holdpoints from cohort selectors and session affinity metadata, maintains per-holdpo...

title: introduce-stream-sanitizer-window-and-functioncall-flush-to-preserve-json-span-contiguity
preview: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents and fuzz tests revealed cases where SSE chunk boundaries could split JSON object spans or function-call sentinel tokens in ways that make downstream parsers emit truncated or mis-ordered events. This caused structured-output consumers and OpenAI-compatibility wrappers to...
text: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents and fuzz tests revealed cases where SSE chunk boundaries could split JSON object spans or function-call sentinel tokens in ways that make downstream parsers emit truncated or mis-ordered events. This caused structured-output consumers and OpenAI-compatibility wrappers to drop function-call arguments or mis-associate tokens when partial frames were replayed. This PR introduces a lightweight streaming sanitizer layer and a small reflow window that: 1) keeps a minimal lookback buffer for boundary repair, 2) normalizes delimiter emission order for function/tool-call sentinels, and 3) applies a safe escape-rewrite for split UTF-8/JSON token fragments. The description below summarizes changes, risks, testing, and rollout plan. Changes made: - runtime/sse/stream_window.go: new StreamSanitizer that maintains a sliding reflow window (configurable m...

title: Policy engine: add priority classes and per-route budgets (deterministic decision logging + validation)
preview: Summary: This PR extends the policy engine to support (a) explicit priority classes (enterprise/standard/best-effort/internal) and (b) per-route budgets that can be consumed deterministically during admission control / overload protection. It also adds deterministic decision logging (stable reason codes + structured...
text: Summary: This PR extends the policy engine to support (a) explicit priority classes (enterprise/standard/best-effort/internal) and (b) per-route budgets that can be consumed deterministically during admission control / overload protection. It also adds deterministic decision logging (stable reason codes + structured fields) and a schema validation layer to keep policies auditable for enterprise escalations. Motivation: Hot-route capacity protection requires a single, explainable place to answer: "given tenant, route group, and current capacity signals, should we admit / throttle / shed / fallback". We need to do this with minimal per-request overhead and without introducing high-cardinality telemetry. This PR focuses on correctness + determinism; performance tuning (memoization/batched lookups) will follow. What’s in scope: 1) Policy schema additions: - priority_class: enum {INTERNAL,...

title: Slab concatenation + FlashMixer kernel with async KV coalescer and continuous-batching horizon
preview: Context: Operator-level profiling in the last quarter showed sustained stalls on small-to-medium sequence lengths due to scatter/gather overheads and suboptimal KV warmup for bursty traffic. This PR introduces a new attention strategy (SlabConcatenate + FlashMixer microkernel) that reduces per-token overhead for het...
text: Context: Operator-level profiling in the last quarter showed sustained stalls on small-to-medium sequence lengths due to scatter/gather overheads and suboptimal KV warmup for bursty traffic. This PR introduces a new attention strategy (SlabConcatenate + FlashMixer microkernel) that reduces per-token overhead for heterogeneous sequence distributions and a lightweight async-KV coalescer that amortizes KV cache population across micro-batches. Motivation: 1) Reduce small-batch latency and increase throughput at mid-sequence lengths (256-2048) where customer workloads spend most of their tokens. 2) Retain deterministic rotary/rope semantics while enabling a fused concatenate path that avoids extra memcpy for tiled KV layouts. 3) Improve continuous batching horizon so runtime can opportunistically stretch batching without violating latency SLOs. What this change does: - Introduces FlashMix...

title: unified tool-caller API + delayed-commit retry semantics to stabilize streaming and multi-tool results
preview: Context and motivation: Customers frequently surface two pain points when composing LLM responses with external tools: 1) streaming instability when tool results and model tokens interleave mid-stream, and 2) duplicate side-effects from naive retry semantics for parallel tool calls. This PR introduces a unified 'Too...
text: Context and motivation: Customers frequently surface two pain points when composing LLM responses with external tools: 1) streaming instability when tool results and model tokens interleave mid-stream, and 2) duplicate side-effects from naive retry semantics for parallel tool calls. This PR introduces a unified 'Tool Caller' API and a delayed-commit retry strategy to address both issues in a developer-friendly, OpenAI-compatible surface. Design summary: The Tool Caller provides a single entrypoint for: a) orchestrating ordered or parallel tool invocations with deterministic merge semantics, b) registering streaming checkpoints so the model can safely stream partial token output without observing incomplete tool side-effects, and c) delayed-commit retries that only expose tool results to the model after idempotency checks pass. Streaming stability is handled via per-tool stream checkpo...

title: Add KMS throttle budget + request backpressure to prevent KMS saturation under spikes
preview: Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope...
text:
Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope ops (e.g. cache cold start + traffic spike) could saturate KMS and extend the incident blast radius.

This PR introduces an explicit "KMS throttle budget" and integrates KMS calls with the existing request admission/backpressure path so we shed/queue work earlier rather than stampeding KMS.

What’s included (high level): (1) Per-process token-bucket budget for KMS operations: A lightweight budget that is decremented on KMS requests and refilled at a configured rate. When depleted, KMS calls are delayed (bounded) or rejected with a typed error depending on caller policy. Th...

title: adaptive qk-kv fusion with prefetching and rotary interleave for long-context throughput
preview: Motivation: Long-context workloads (>=16k tokens) dominate several customer workloads. Existing FlashAttention and separate QK/KV kernels yield suboptimal memory traffic when serving long sequences with continuous batching and active KV caching. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel strategy that fuses QK compute an...
text: Motivation: Long-context workloads (>=16k tokens) dominate several customer workloads. Existing FlashAttention and separate QK/KV kernels yield suboptimal memory traffic when serving long sequences with continuous batching and active KV caching. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel strategy that fuses QK compute and KV accumulation paths where beneficial, plus a lightweight KV prefetcher and a rotary-interleave layout to reduce memory bandwidth and kernel launch overhead. Design: 1) New fused kernels (CUDA) that compute QK scores and simultaneously write/aggregate KV fragments for future decode steps. These kernels are selected when sequence-length, batch composition, and kv cache hit heuristics indicate net win. 2) Adaptive kernel dispatcher: runtime heuristics (histogram of seq lengths, batch fill factor, GPU occupancy) choose between FlashAttention variants, fused QK-KV path, or t...

title: decouple-serializer-from-affinity-and-introduce-cooperative-serialize-queues
preview: Motivation: our telemetry and recent incident traces show a non-trivial CPU-side bottleneck in the request hotpath caused by blocking serialization work that holds thread-affinity (OS-level pinning) and prevents efficient cooperative scheduling between IO, serializer, and runtime workers. This PR separates the conce...
text: Motivation: our telemetry and recent incident traces show a non-trivial CPU-side bottleneck in the request hotpath caused by blocking serialization work that holds thread-affinity (OS-level pinning) and prevents efficient cooperative scheduling between IO, serializer, and runtime workers. This PR separates the concerns: (1) introduce a lightweight cooperative serialization queue that runs on worker threads without requiring permanent thread pinning, (2) decouple the serializer state machine from thread-affinity so short critical sections can opportunistically run on any available worker, and (3) add backpressure signals to the queue which feed into the scheduler to dynamically throttle batch assembly when CPU saturation is detected. Design summary: add runtime/serialize_queue.{h,cpp} implementing a fixed-size ring queue with lock-free handover for small payloads and a CAS-protected ov...

title: Introduce lightweight tracepoint sampler and batch-pressure microbench for scheduler visibility
preview: Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to correlate scheduler decisions, KV-cache hot prefixes, and continuous-batching pressure in production-like workloads. Existing NVTX and perfetto exports are useful but either too heavy or too coarse for iterative scheduler tuning. This PR introduces a lightweight tracepoint s...
text:
Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to correlate scheduler decisions, KV-cache hot prefixes, and continuous-batching pressure in production-like workloads. Existing NVTX and perfetto exports are useful but either too heavy or too coarse for iterative scheduler tuning. This PR introduces a lightweight tracepoint sampler plus a compact microbench harness that reproduces batch-pressure scenarios seen in customer workloads.

What this PR does: 1) Introduces a TracepointSampler module (C++): periodic, low-payload tracepoints emitted around scheduler windowing and key KV-cache touchpoints. Sampling is rate-limited and context-tagged so it is safe to enable in Dedicated/Private pockets for short experiments. 2) Adds a BatchPressureMicrobench: a small harness that simulates mixed arrival patterns (burst+poisson), tracks batch fill/evict cycles, and reports per-step metrics (queue depth, av...

title: Adaptive kernel routing and KV-scheduler tweaks for Mistral/Qwen (GQA/MQA tuning)
preview: Motivation: production workloads showed inconsistent latency/throughput tradeoffs across model families (Mistral, Qwen, Llama variants) when using a single kernel selection and KV cache routing policy. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel routing layer and a lightweight KV-scheduler that chooses sharding and prefet...
text: Motivation: production workloads showed inconsistent latency/throughput tradeoffs across model families (Mistral, Qwen, Llama variants) when using a single kernel selection and KV cache routing policy. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel routing layer and a lightweight KV-scheduler that chooses sharding and prefetch priorities per model and request workload, plus targeted GQA/MQA tuning knobs. The goals are: reduce p50/p95 latency variance, increase sustained throughput for mixed-sequence traffic, and avoid tail regressions when switching model variants. Summary of changes (single-line condensed): implement model profiles for kernel selection and quantization-aware algorithms; add KV-scheduler with per-route priority and prefetch window; continuous-batching changes to respect model affinity and early-flush heuristics; small Llama-specific attention correction for 8-bit MQA flows; Qw...

title: scheduler: jittered slot reassignment and KV-warmup throttling
preview: Context: In high-concurrency mixed workloads we observed correlated slot reclamations and KV cache warmups causing transient starvation and p95 latency spikes. This PR implements two complementary runtime controls: (1) jittered slot reassignment to spread slot release/reassignment events across short randomized wind...
text: Context: In high-concurrency mixed workloads we observed correlated slot reclamations and KV cache warmups causing transient starvation and p95 latency spikes. This PR implements two complementary runtime controls: (1) jittered slot reassignment to spread slot release/reassignment events across short randomized windows (configurable), and (2) a KV-warmup throttler that staggers cold-KV prefetches and enforces a soft admission budget per-slot to avoid simultaneous heavy prefetches. Changes: Implements a new scheduler helper (jitter_reassign) which inserts small randomized offsets before slot handoff and coordinates with the slot allocator to avoid churn; implements kv_warmup_throttle which tracks per-model warmup tokens and decays budgets based on successful cache hits; exposes scheduler.tuning.jitter_ms and kv.warmup.budget_rate config knobs; updates continuous_batcher admission path ...

title: Reduce CPU syscalls and thread handoff on request path
preview: Motivation: CPU-side bottlenecks on the request hot path were dominated by frequent syscalls and expensive thread handoffs when requests crossed the network/IO boundary. This PR reduces syscall frequency and simplifies handoff between acceptor/dispatcher threads and worker pools to lower tail latency and overall CPU...
text: Motivation: CPU-side bottlenecks on the request hot path were dominated by frequent syscalls and expensive thread handoffs when requests crossed the network/IO boundary. This PR reduces syscall frequency and simplifies handoff between acceptor/dispatcher threads and worker pools to lower tail latency and overall CPU usage. Summary of approach: 1) Replace a large fraction of blocking read()/write()/select() syscalls on the hot path with an edge-triggered epoll-based poll loop + per-core submission queues to amortize kernel transitions; 2) Introduce a light-weight per-core MPMC ring buffer (lock-free where supported) used to hand off ready requests to CPU-local worker shards instead of parking/unparking threads; 3) Move request serialization (marshalling) off the acceptor path onto a short-lived serialization worker that batches small requests together (continuous batching window = 1.5m...

title: Introduce jurisdiction-aware assertion proxy, KMS consent router, and audit enrichment shim
preview: Motivation: customers with mixed-residency fleets asked for a deterministic place to evaluate operator/service jurisdiction assertions so KMS usage and audit provenance can be routed safely without sprinkling logic across services. This PR introduces an assertion proxy (service: residency-assertor) that normalizes i...
text: Motivation: customers with mixed-residency fleets asked for a deterministic place to evaluate operator/service jurisdiction assertions so KMS usage and audit provenance can be routed safely without sprinkling logic across services. This PR introduces an assertion proxy (service: residency-assertor) that normalizes incoming claims, evaluates configured jurisdiction policies, and emits a compact proof token used by downstream KMS routing and audit enrichment. It also adds a KMS Consent Router to pick an appropriate CMK/region and a lightweight audit-enrichment shim that attaches pseudonymous residency and proof metadata to audit events. Implementation summary: 1) residency-assertor service (new binary) with a small policy DSL converted into OPA-compatible JSON; 2) kms-consent-router middleware integrated into the existing kms-proxy client path with a decision cache and TTL; 3) audit-enr...

title: token-span-attribution-and-adaptive-alert-smoothing
preview:
Motivation:
We currently surface token-level cost and latency aggregations but lack a clear way to attribute SLO impact to specific token spans (e.g. model prompt prefix vs generated tail) and we get frequent alert flapping on percentile SLOs when low-volume, high-cost tails show up. This PR introduces two complemen...
text:
Motivation:
We currently surface token-level cost and latency aggregations but lack a clear way to attribute SLO impact to specific token spans (e.g. model prompt prefix vs generated tail) and we get frequent alert flapping on percentile SLOs when low-volume, high-cost tails show up. This PR introduces two complementary capabilities: 1) span-level token attribution traces and rollups keyed by token position buckets (prefix/mid/suffix) and cache-affinity (kv-hit vs kv-miss); and 2) an adaptive alert-smoothing layer that reduces noise for route-level token-cost SLOs using a decayed-reservoir quantile estimator that weights tokens by cost and recency.

What this changes (high level):
- Runtime telemetry: emit token-level spans with attributes: token_index, span_bucket (prefix|mid|suffix), kv_affinity (hit|miss), token_cost_units. These are synthesized as aggregated metrics and lightweigh...

title: Introduce policy simulator with KMS proofing and audit-diff tooling
preview: Title: Introduce policy simulator with KMS proofing and audit-diff tooling. Motivation: Customers and internal auditors frequently ask for a way to validate RBAC v2 policy changes before rollout and to produce cryptographic proof that a proposed change does not widen region/KMS residency exposure. This PR adds a dev...
text: Title: Introduce policy simulator with KMS proofing and audit-diff tooling. Motivation: Customers and internal auditors frequently ask for a way to validate RBAC v2 policy changes before rollout and to produce cryptographic proof that a proposed change does not widen region/KMS residency exposure. This PR adds a developer-facing simulator (rbac-sim) that evaluates a set of example requests against a candidate policy delta, emits a human- and machine-readable audit-diff, and (optionally) attaches a short-lived KMS-signed proof bundle that attests to the simulator inputs and outputs for downstream compliance workflows. This is intended to reduce post-deploy surprises, speed review cycles for Dedicated/Private deployments, and plug into our audit retention processes. Summary of changes (high level commits): 1) feat(simulator): introduce rbac-sim CLI and library with JSON policy delta app...

title: heterogeneous-attention-fusion-with-staged-scheduler-and-continuous-batching-support
preview: Motivation: reduce p99 latency regression for mixed-sequence workloads and improve throughput for production chat and retrieval runtimes by selecting optimal attention kernels at runtime and enabling continuous batching without sacrificing KV cache hit stability. Changes: Introduces a heterogeneous attention selecti...
text: Motivation: reduce p99 latency regression for mixed-sequence workloads and improve throughput for production chat and retrieval runtimes by selecting optimal attention kernels at runtime and enabling continuous batching without sacrificing KV cache hit stability. Changes: Introduces a heterogeneous attention selection layer (HeteroAttn) that picks between a low-latency microkernel and a high-throughput Flash-like kernel based on sequence length heuristics and current queueing pressure; adds a staged scheduler that enforces a short-term reservation window for latency-sensitive requests and a batch-aggregation window for throughput requests; implements a cooperative KV cache eviction policy (staged LRU) that works with continuous batching to avoid repeated cache thrash during rollups; adds fused rotary/rope offset precomputation paths that avoid memcpy for common rotary patterns; integr...

title: introduce-auth-tactician-ttl-prefetch-and-pullstream-refactor
preview: Context: Cross-SDK follow-up to earlier session/lease work — introduces an "auth tactician" component that centralizes token TTL awareness and a small prefetch policy to avoid tail latency for streamed pull/push APIs. Motivation: customers saw intermittent ~200-400ms spikes when long-running streams triggered last-s...
text: Context: Cross-SDK follow-up to earlier session/lease work — introduces an "auth tactician" component that centralizes token TTL awareness and a small prefetch policy to avoid tail latency for streamed pull/push APIs. Motivation: customers saw intermittent ~200-400ms spikes when long-running streams triggered last-second token refreshes; retry/backoff logic masked this but increased request churn and observable noise. Goal: reduce tail latency by proactively warming credentials within a TTL window, provide a small, well-documented opt-in prefetch policy across Python/TS/Go SDKs, and clean up PullStream ergonomics so the language SDKs expose a common surface for cancellable/asynchronous consumption. What I changed: 1) auth/tactician: new minimal stateful component (Python: redwood_sdk.session.tactician, TS: auth/tactician.ts, Go: session/tactician.go) that tracks token TTLs, schedules ...

title: Tokenwise scale-lowering and ring-buffer collation for quantized kernels
preview: Summary: Adds a tokenwise scale-lowering compiler pass and a ring-buffer collation stage in the runtime to reduce per-token quant/dequant churn and to coalesce KV cache writes from continuous-batching. Motivation: some workloads with short prefixes and frequent small continuations were suffering from per-token scale...
text:
Summary: Adds a tokenwise scale-lowering compiler pass and a ring-buffer collation stage in the runtime to reduce per-token quant/dequant churn and to coalesce KV cache writes from continuous-batching. Motivation: some workloads with short prefixes and frequent small continuations were suffering from per-token scale conversions and tight kernel launches that prevented microbatch coalescing on INT8 and FP8 execution paths. This PR targets the hot path by lowering scale operations earlier and grouping adjacent KV writes into a ring-backed collator so the GPU-side kernel sees larger contiguous work items without changing model numerics.

What changed (high-level):
- New compiler pass runtime/quant/scale_lowering.cc that detects consecutive token-scale operations and emits a lowered tokenwise scale applied as a fused kernel input where safe. The pass is careful to preserve per-head scalin...

title: adaptive-kernel-selector-and-prefetch-degradation-policy
preview: Motivation: recent incidents showed increased p99 latency spikes when the runtime switched kernels aggressively for short sequences and simultaneously attempted speculative KV prefetching. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel selection policy plus a controlled prefetch degradation path that trades a small amount of...
text:
Motivation: recent incidents showed increased p99 latency spikes when the runtime switched kernels aggressively for short sequences and simultaneously attempted speculative KV prefetching. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel selection policy plus a controlled prefetch degradation path that trades a small amount of throughput for deterministic correctness and lower tail latency under mixed workloads.

What this does (single-string description): Adds a KernelSelector that scores candidate kernels by estimated latency, numerical-safety score, and cache-friendliness; introduces a fallback budget token that bounds how often the fast path can be taken per model+device pair; implements a PrefetchDegradationPolicy that steps down speculative KV prefetch aggressiveness when p95/p99 latency or error-rate thresholds are exceeded; exposes a runtime flag --safe-mode to force conservative kernel ...

title: kv-prefetch-aware-batching-with-sparse-scratch-allocator
preview: Context: In production workloads with long chat histories the KV cache dominates transient GPU workspace allocations and forces arena growth during batching spikes. Motivation: Reduce peak GPU footprint while preserving latency SLOs and steady-state throughput. Summary: Introduces a KV-prefetch hotset buffer and a s...
text: Context: In production workloads with long chat histories the KV cache dominates transient GPU workspace allocations and forces arena growth during batching spikes. Motivation: Reduce peak GPU footprint while preserving latency SLOs and steady-state throughput. Summary: Introduces a KV-prefetch hotset buffer and a sparse scratch allocator to avoid inflating the global arena during continuous batching. The hotset prefetcher tracks request-level KV hotness and pre-populates a compact per-device hotset capable of serving the majority of cache hits without expanding the scratch arena. The sparse scratch allocator exposes a lightweight free-list for short-lived activation fragments and returns large contiguous ranges back to the arena quickly, reducing fragmentation. Key changes: 1) runtime/kv: prefetch_hotset component with heuristics for prefix/predictive prefetch and bounded memory cap....

title: introduce-tenant-kms-binding-enforcer-and-audit-linker
preview: Motivation: We need a stronger guardrail that prevents cross-tenant KMS usage in multi-tenant Dedicated/Private deployments while preserving operator ergonomics and auditability. This PR implements a runtime enforcer that ensures KMS key bindings are validated against the request's tenant/namespace claims before all...
text: Motivation: We need a stronger guardrail that prevents cross-tenant KMS usage in multi-tenant Dedicated/Private deployments while preserving operator ergonomics and auditability. This PR implements a runtime enforcer that ensures KMS key bindings are validated against the request's tenant/namespace claims before allowing encrypt/decrypt/sign operations. It also introduces an audit linker service that enriches existing audit events with KMS provenance (key id, provider, binding-check-result) and a correlation id that ties application requests to KMS calls for post-incident investigation. Design summary: (1) policy-operator plugin that evaluates declared kms.bindings for a tenant namespace and caches validated bindings in a short-lived policy store; (2) request-time gate in the kms-proxy that checks the caller's tenant claim against the policy store and denies calls that violate binding...

title: Add KMS metrics + tracing spans for standardized wrapper calls
preview: Summary: Instruments all KMS operations invoked via the new shared wrapper with metrics and trace spans following Redwood telemetry conventions. Adds latency histograms, request/error counters (tagged by canonical error taxonomy), and a consistent tracing span shape so on-call can correlate KMS degradation to elevat...
text: Summary: Instruments all KMS operations invoked via the new shared wrapper with metrics and trace spans following Redwood telemetry conventions. Adds latency histograms, request/error counters (tagged by canonical error taxonomy), and a consistent tracing span shape so on-call can correlate KMS degradation to elevated 5xx/latency. Motivation: As we harden KMS integration paths (ENG-4821), we need consistent KMS dependency signals across control-plane and data-plane components. Recent support cases and a throttling-related incident (SUP-19377) showed we lacked enough granularity to quickly answer: (1) which operation is failing, (2) whether it’s retry amplification, (3) what error class dominates, and (4) which region/provider is impacted. This PR builds on pr-18421’s wrapper API and wires metrics/spans at the wrapper boundary (single place, consistent tags). What’s included: Metrics (...

title: Cost‑Smarts: telemetry-driven prefill scheduler and quantization hint adapter
preview: Context and motivation: Large parts of our cost-automation stack rely on static prefill and quantization defaults that are tuned per-model but not per-workload. Customers running mixed telemetry (short chat bursts + long completion bursts) see suboptimal unit economics because prefill and quant decisions are not bei...
text:
Context and motivation: Large parts of our cost-automation stack rely on static prefill and quantization defaults that are tuned per-model but not per-workload. Customers running mixed telemetry (short chat bursts + long completion bursts) see suboptimal unit economics because prefill and quant decisions are not being informed by runtime signals. This PR introduces 'Cost‑Smarts' — a telemetry-driven prefill scheduler plus a quantization hint adapter that surfaces unit-econ deltas into the serving runtime policy engine.

What this change does: Implements a small, runtime-side scheduler that consumes existing request telemetry (sequence length histograms, prefix-kv hit ratios, tail-latency percentile) and emits short-lived prefill decisions (aggressive/standard/disabled) for a given route+model tuple. In parallel, the quantization hint adapter exposes a per-route score representing the ...

title: Dedicated autoscaler: add metrics + structured logs for desired vs bounded capacity and prediction error
preview: Context: As part of Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode, we need auditability and operability around what the autoscaler *wanted* to do vs what it actually did after applying customer bounds (min/max) and safety invariants. This PR adds first-class metrics and structured logs for (1) requested desired rep...
text:
Context: As part of Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode, we need auditability and operability around what the autoscaler *wanted* to do vs what it actually did after applying customer bounds (min/max) and safety invariants. This PR adds first-class metrics and structured logs for (1) requested desired replicas vs bounded desired replicas, (2) bound-hit counters (clamped to min/max), (3) prediction error and staleness when predictive mode is enabled, and (4) an explicit scale-event reason enum that shows up in both metrics and logs.

What’s in this PR:
- New gauge metrics (emitted every autoscaler tick per deployment/region):
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.desired_replicas (the raw desired output from the decision function)
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.bounded_desired_replicas (after applying min/max + safety floors)
 - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.current_replicas
 - re...
